match.userMessage:
9-panel cinematic storyboard sheet derived from Image 1. Mix Wide, Medium, and Tight shots of the same scene. No borders between panels.

Layout: 3x3 grid (9 panels) in a 16:9 landscape image.

Scene: Modern social-first product photography with tactile editorial energy captured in 16:9, SCARLETT in clean white tank top with coral lipstick already applied sits at a crisp white vanity in a minimal neutral studio, leaning forward toward a microphone with her right hand extended mid-reach to lift a slim coral lipstick tube from its open minimal box, fingers just touching the matte surface with potential energy frozen in the moment muscles tensed and gaze directed slightly off-camera, bright natural window light pouring across the vanity creating clean directional shadows and crisp whites against soft beige tones throughout the sparse background, shallow depth of field with the hand and lipstick tube in sharp focus while the rest of the neutral studio softly blurs, high-key airy atmosphere with true-to-life color and a single pop of coral accent, eye-level slightly above angle with natural lens quality and minimal processing.

Match Image 1 exactly: same character(s) (face, hair, skin, clothing), lighting, color grade, and texture. If character reference sheets are provided, use them for likeness — do not render them as separate panels.

No text, dialogue bubbles, scene numbers, or watermarks.


  <reference-images>
    - Image 1 [PRIMARY SOURCE]: Primary source scene — generate 9 variant shots from this image
    - Image 2 [CHARACTER REF]: SCARLETT - Match the real-world appearance of Sienna Blake exactly
    - Image 3 [LOCATION REF]: INT. BONDI STUDIO - MORNING - Bright window light pours across a crisp white vanity in a minimal neutral studio space
    
    IMPORTANT: Character, location, and element reference images are for IDENTITY CONSISTENCY ONLY. Do NOT reproduce them as separate panels or subjects. Elements (logos, products) must render faithfully when referenced but should appear naturally within the scene. All output panels must depict the scene from the PRIMARY SOURCE image.
  </reference-images>
